title: unexpected "Unsized numbers/parameters not allowed in concatenations."
body:
Tested on latest git. Link to example source code:
https://github.com/jeras/miscelaneous/tree/main/verilator_localparam_in_struct_init

I defined a packed structure type:

```SystemVerilog
    typedef struct packed {
        int unsigned ADDR_WIDTH;
        int unsigned DATA_WIDTH;
        int unsigned CODE_WIDTH;
    } phy_t;
```

Which I use to define a `localparam`.

```SystemVerilog
    localparam phy_t PHY1 = '{
        ADDR_WIDTH: 12,
        DATA_WIDTH: 32,
        CODE_WIDTH: 1024
    };
```

Which works properly, but if I replace the literals with a `localparam int unsigned XLEN = 32` defined before.

```SystemVerilog
    localparam phy_t PHY2 = '{
        ADDR_WIDTH: 20,
        DATA_WIDTH: 32,
        CODE_WIDTH: XLEN
    };
```

I get a warning (warning is from linked example code):

```sh
$ ./build-verilator.sh 
%Warning-WIDTHCONCAT: verilator_localparam_in_struct_init.sv:20:9: Unsized numbers/parameters not allowed in concatenations.
                                                                 : ... note: In instance 'verilator_localparam_in_struct_init'
   20 |         CODE_WIDTH: XLEN
      |         ^~~~~~~~~~
                      ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHCONCAT?v=5.035
                      ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHCONCAT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-WIDTHCONCAT: verilator_localparam_in_struct_init.sv:25:9: Unsized numbers/parameters not allowed in concatenations.
                                                                 : ... note: In instance 'verilator_localparam_in_struct_init'
   25 |         DATA_WIDTH: 64,
      |         ^~~~~~~~~~
%Warning-WIDTHCONCAT: verilator_localparam_in_struct_init.sv:32:9: Unsized numbers/parameters not allowed in concatenations.
                                                                 : ... note: In instance 'verilator_localparam_in_struct_init'
   32 |         DATA_WIDTH: int'(XLEN),
      |         ^~~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 3 warning(s)
```

The position of the warning depends on the mix of literals and XLEN, the linked example tests 4 variations.

Also this is not a concatenation but a **structure literal**, if this part of the Verilator code understands the distinction (since structured parameter code is not functionally complete) the warning text should be changed.

Using `/* verilator lint_off WIDTHCONCAT */` removes the warning, but there should be no warning (for reference I tested using `questa` and Vivado simulator).
